title: Co-owner of Maine lumber mill dies weeks after fire, explosion that killed 2 others
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Alden J. Robbins, co-owner of Robbins Lumber in Searsmont, Maine, died from injuries sustained in a May 15 explosion.
The explosion at the lumber mill also killed firefighter Andrew Cross and Assistant Chief Wayne Woodbury, increasing the death toll to three.
The cause of the explosion, deemed accidental, involved rapid ignition of particulate material at the base of a silo.

title: Plymouth DA seeks assault and battery charge against Brockton mayor
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Plymouth DA's office filed for a clerk-magistrate hearing on an assault charge against Brockton Mayor Moises Rodrigues.
The application follows a student's claim Rodrigues inappropriately touched her during a school parade on May 22.
A judge previously declined to extend a harassment order against Rodrigues but called his conduct 'unwelcome' and 'unprofessional.'

title: Police searching for tandem moped riders after shots fired in Roxbury
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston police are searching for two suspects involved in a Roxbury shots-fired incident on June 30.
The suspects were last seen riding a tandem moped down Schuyler Street toward Elm Hill Avenue, wearing black masks.
Police urge anyone with information about the incident to contact detectives at (617) 343-4275.

title: Jaylen Brown comforts 6-year-old fan whose reaction to trade went viral
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown met with 6-year-old fan Giovanni Jean at M.I.T. following his emotional reaction to Brown's trade.
Giovanni's reaction to Brown being traded to the 76ers received over 1.1 million likes on Instagram.
During the meeting, Brown received a handwritten letter from Giovanni and reassured him of their friendship.

title: Fatal Cambridge shooting renews criticism of council vote to end ShotSpotter contract
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was shot and killed in Cambridge on July 4, with his body found an hour later.
The shooting reignited debate over Cambridge's decision to end its ShotSpotter contract that detected gunshots.
Cambridge officials and police unions claim ShotSpotter could have hastened emergency response to Bautista's shooting.

title: Jaylen Brown on Celtics trade: Boston is home and no organization can take that away
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown continues community work in Boston despite trade to the 76ers, launching 7uice Foundation's summer program.
Massachusetts Governor Maura Healey declared July 7 as 7uice Foundation Day, honoring Brown's impact on the community.
Brown, a five-time NBA All-Star, joined the 76ers, teaming up with Joel Embiid following a 10-year stint with the Celtics.

title: Wrong-way I-95 driver faces drunk driving charges after crash, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: John Tarshi, 25, of Lawrence, faces drunk driving charges after crashing a Porsche on I-95 in Woburn.
Police suspect a forged New Hampshire RMV document in the vehicle, potentially leading to additional charges.
The wrong-way incident follows a crackdown on impaired and wrong-way driving across Massachusetts after recent fatalities.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming now (July 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jennifer Garner stars in 'The Five-Star Weekend,' debuting on Peacock July 9, alongside Regina Hall and Chloë Sevigny.
A24's movie 'The Drama,' filmed in Boston, will premiere on HBO Max on July 31.
HBO Max adds shark content for Shark Week starting July 26; 'Jaws' series re-added to Peacock.

title: Noah Kahan’s historic 4-show Fenway Park run kicks off Tuesday. Here’s everything you need to know.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan starts a historic four-show run at Fenway Park, with performances on July 18, 2024.
Each night features fireworks; set times include 8 p.m. for Kahan, preceded by Annabelle Dinda and Gigi Perez.
Local businesses offer Kahan-themed items, with proceeds benefiting his mental health initiative, The Busyhead Project.

title: Labouré College cuts 65 jobs ahead of Curry College transition
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Labouré College in Milton to lay off 65 staff as it closes ahead of Curry College acquisition.
Curry College to take over Labouré's nursing programs and establish the Labouré Center for Advancing Healthcare Opportunity.
Labouré's current students can complete degrees at Curry; 15 faculty and 20 staff may join Curry College.
